content: The neural synchrony-activated long-term working memory retention mechanism for human long-horizon active learning can inspire a hybrid Memory Network architecture that dynamically activates slices of long-term network memory instead of sustaining full short-term storage buffers. This dynamic activation approach reduces memory overhead for long-horizon sequential active learning tasks while preserving the contextual continuity required for coherent reasoning across extended timelines.
source_topics: Working memory retention mechanisms for long-horizon active learning
trigger_topic: Working memory retention mechanisms for long-horizon active learning
